source_jobs.title: Footwear Developer, Women’s
source_jobs.description:
FOOTWEAR DEVELOPER, WOMEN’S

San Francisco, CA

At Rothy’s, we know there’s a better way to do business, and it starts by putting the planet and its people first. More than 225 million single-use plastic bottles and 715,000 pounds of ocean-bound marine plastic have been transformed into our signature thread, creating wardrobe staples that look just as good as they feel.



Striving for zero waste, we combine 3D knitting technology and handcrafted assembly to create machine washable styles that don’t compromise on comfort. From classic shoes to carry-everywhere bags, we create essentials for wherever you go.



At Rothy’s, building a sustainable future is at the heart of everything we do. We pride ourselves on fostering an inclusive environment at our HQ, retail stores and wholly-owned factory, and are growing our community every day.




ABOUT THE TEAM:

Our Production team is always one step ahead. While many teams are responsible for responding to moments in the present, this one looks toward the future. From finessing the production of our silhouettes to developing new materials, our production gurus work hand-in-hand with our factory team, sustainability, merchandising and design partners to make sure the wheels of Rothy’s keep turning on our quest for a more sustainable future.




ABOUT THE ROLE:

Rothy’s is looking for a creative and analytical Footwear Developer to support the development of innovative, high-quality footwear that delights customers. This role is hands-on and highly collaborative, partnering closely with Product Design, Merchandising, Production, and our factory teams to bring footwear from concept through commercialization. The Footwear Developer plays a key role in executing development milestones, ensuring technical integrity, cost targets, and timelines are met.




WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Collaborate with Product Design, Merchandising, and factory partners to develop footwear from concept through commercialization, supporting alignment to design intent, quality standards, cost targets, and timelines

 - Evaluate bottoms, components, upper patterns, knitting, materials, and constructions from fit, performance, durability, costing, and manufacturing feasibility perspectives

 - Provide technical input and identify potential risks early in the development process, proposing alternative solutions as needed

 - Support resolution of product development issues by partnering cross-functionally to deliver cost-effective and timely solutions

 - Analyze cost differences across prototypes and sample stages; review CBDs, yields, and material usage, and support cost negotiations to achieve margin goals

 - Partner with integrity and quality teams to support fit consistency, comfort, and performance from development through production

 - Evaluate materials and components for physical properties, performance, sustainability, and cost-effectiveness to ensure appropriate application

 - Participate as a key contributor in all sample reviews (prototype, development, fit, confirmation, TOP) to support quality and executional consistency

 - Help coordinate and contribute to cross-functional development meetings to review progress, align on decisions, and support on-time delivery

 - Maintain clear, proactive communication with factory and vendor partners throughout development and production

 - Identify opportunities to improve product integrity, development efficiency, and cost savings, contributing ideas and solutions to the broader team




YOU ARE:

 - Able to interpret design intent and product needs with growing independence and technical confidence

 - A self-starter who thrives in a fast-paced, growth-oriented environment and is comfortable learning on the fly

 - Collaborative, solutions-oriented, and eager to partner across teams

 - Curious, adaptable, and open to feedback and new ways of working

 - A clear and effective communicator with internal and external partners

 - Detail-oriented with strong organ

source_jobs.title: Store Manager - Bridgeport Village
source_jobs.description:
STORE MANAGER

Tigard, OR - Bridgeport Village

At Rothy’s, we believe there’s a better way to do business—one that puts the planet and its people first. That’s why we’ve transformed millions of plastic bottles into stylish, sustainable shoes and bags, creating wardrobe staples that look just as good as they feel.

In our retail stores, clienteling, and genuine connection are at the heart of everything we do. We take pride in building lasting relationships with our customers—getting to know their style, anticipating their needs, and creating personalized experiences that make them feel truly seen and supported. Whether it’s helping someone find their perfect fit or sharing the story behind our sustainable designs, every interaction is an opportunity to deepen that connection.

As we grow, we’re cultivating not just a workplace, but a community. It’s a place where careers can grow, ideas are shared, and people feel a true sense of belonging. Every day brings new opportunities to learn, lead, and make an impact—together, as part of a welcoming, inclusive team that celebrates each other’s contributions.




ABOUT THE TEAM:

Shopping at Rothy’s IRL is not your ordinary retail experience—so naturally, our Stores team is extraordinary, too. Experts in the fit, wear and sizing of our shoes, these team members provide deep knowledge of every Rothy’s style and out-of-this-world customer service—creating long-lasting brand loyalty. Customers love shopping in our stores due to these exceptional interactions.




ABOUT THE ROLE:

As Store Manager, you create an exceptional customer experience while keeping daily operations running smoothly. You lead a high-performing, engaged team and ensure the store operates efficiently. You collaborate with other stores and departments to solve problems, drive improvements, and set your team up for success. As a Rothy’s brand ambassador, you enthusiastically bring the mission and values to life for our customers, employees, and community.




WHAT YOU’LL DO:

Team Leadership (35%)

 - Proactively recruit, hire, onboard and develop a strong store team

 - Develop team members through in-the-moment feedback, regular floor observations, and consistent 1:1 check-ins

 - Maintain a high performance standard by training the team on all SOPs, service expectations, selling skills, and Rothy's product knowledge

 - Lead the Division of Responsibility (DOR) program with your leadership team

 - Ensure consistent execution of the Manager on Duty (MOD) program by setting clear expectations, reinforcing accountability, and regularly reviewing performance to deliver seamless customer and employee experience

 - Coach for high performance and manage employee relations concerns in partnership with your supervisor and the People Team

 - Create a positive and motivating team culture by actively identifying and acting on engagement opportunities

Customer Experience (50%)

 - Lead and model Rothy’s Experience through exceptional service and selling from the sales floor, ensuring the team is fully service-certified and actively coaching, training, and inspecting behaviors to uphold brand standards

 - Build customer loyalty through effective clienteling, working together with the team to strengthen long term relationships

 - Be the store's expert on brand and product knowledge, ensuring the team confidently shares our style, fit, quality, and sustainability story

 - Drive sales and key performance indicators by setting clear goals, celebrating wins, and holding the team accountable through daily coaching

 - Plan and execute in-store events and activations that grow brand awareness, connect with the local community, and drive sales

 - Ensure all presentation touchpoints including visual merchandising, cleanliness, and dress code consistently reflect brand standards and optimize the in-store experience

Operational Excellence (15%)

 - Plan and execute weekly, monthly, and annual business strategies to hit sales growth

source_jobs.title: Chief Financial Officer
source_jobs.description:
Chief Financial Officer (CFO)
San Francisco, CA

At Rothy’s, we know there’s a better way to do business, and it starts by putting the planet and its people first. More than 225 million single-use plastic bottles and 715,000 pounds of ocean-bound marine plastic have been transformed into our signature thread, creating wardrobe staples that look just as good as they feel.

Striving for zero waste, we combine 3D knitting technology and handcrafted assembly to create machine washable styles that don’t compromise on comfort. From classic shoes to carry-everywhere bags, we create essentials for wherever you go.

At Rothy’s, building a sustainable future is at the heart of everything we do. We pride ourselves on fostering an inclusive environment at our HQ, retail stores and wholly-owned factory, and are growing our community every day.

The Role
The CFO is the CEO’s key financial and strategic partner. This leader owns all aspects of Finance, Accounting, and Strategy, helping the company scale globally with strong financial discipline. The CFO will guide long-term planning, capital allocation, investor relations, and Board communication, ensuring the business has the visibility and financial structure needed to grow profitably.

This role requires a hands-on operator with the ability to balance long-term strategic planning with day-to-day execution, ensuring we deliver consistent growth and operational excellence.

What you’ll do:

 - Lead all Finance and Accounting functions, ensuring accuracy, compliance, and timely reporting. Develop an all-star team

 - Partner closely with the CEO to set company strategy and ensure alignment across the executive team and broader organization

 - Build scalable financial systems, processes, and controls to support global, multi-channel growth

 - Oversee budgeting, forecasting, cash flow planning, and capital allocation

 - Develop financial metrics and dashboards that enable clear performance management

 - Lead investor relations and Board communication

 - Partner closely with cross-functional leaders to evaluate the business, new opportunities, partnerships, and global expansion

 - Serve as a key voice with the Board of Directors, investors, and other external stakeholders, alongside the CEO

 - Champion a culture of collaboration, transparency, and high performance across the company

You have:

 - 15+ years of progressive leadership experience in Finance in consumer goods, retail, or adjacent industries

 - Proven track record as a CFO in a fast-scaling, global, multi-channel business

 - Deep financial expertise, including financial planning, forecasting, capital allocation, and performance management

 - MBA and/or CPA preferred

 - Investor relations and Board communication experience

 - Business development or M&A experience preferred, including deal evaluation, integration planning, or partnership development

 - Excellent communication, influence, and leadership skills, with ability to operate as a trusted partner to the CEO and Board

 - High integrity, low ego, and a collaborative leadership style

Our benefits:

 - Generous paid time off

 - 401k matching

 - Comprehensive health plans for you and your family

 - Supplemental mental health benefits

 - Monthly wellness reimbursement

 - Dog Friendly Offices

 - Employee Discount Program!

source_jobs.title: Seasonal Retail Ambassador - Fashion Island
source_jobs.description:
SEASONAL RETAIL AMBASSADOR – FASHION ISLAND

Location: Newport Beach, CA – Fashion Island

 

At Rothy’s, we believe there’s a better way to do business—one that puts the planet and its people first. That’s why we’ve transformed millions of plastic bottles into stylish, sustainable shoes and bags, creating wardrobe staples that look just as good as they feel.

 

In our retail stores, clienteling and genuine connection are at the heart of everything we do. We take pride in building lasting relationships with our customers—getting to know their style, anticipating their needs, and creating personalized experiences that make them feel truly seen and supported. As a Seasonal Retail Ambassador, you will help us deliver these exceptional experiences during our busiest periods while supporting our inclusive and welcoming Fashion Island store community.

 


ABOUT THE TEAM

Shopping at Rothy’s IRL is not your ordinary retail experience—so naturally, our Stores team is extraordinary. Our team members are experts in fit, style, and the story behind our products. They share in our mission and are dedicated to providing extraordinary customer service and building long-lasting brand loyalty.

 


ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Seasonal Retail Ambassador, you’ll represent the Rothy’s brand on the sales floor during peak seasons. You’ll focus on delivering memorable customer service, fostering meaningful connections, and supporting store operations. Your enthusiasm for sustainable fashion and commitment to service will help create a positive shopping experience for every customer.

 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Deliver best-in-class customer service rooted in Rothy’s values, consistently embodying the brand in every interaction.

 - Drive personal and team sales results by engaging with customers to understand needs and share product expertise.

 - Create lasting relationships with customers through strong clienteling practices.

 - Contribute to a positive, inclusive team culture by modeling collaborative, service-driven behavior.

 - Support daily store operations, including stock replenishment, visual merchandising, and store organization.

 - Follow all store policies and standard operating procedures to ensure operational excellence and compliance.

 


YOU ARE

 - Guided by Rothy’s values and dedicated to fostering a welcoming, service-driven environment.

 - Excited to embrace possibility, innovate, make the planet proud, and work collaboratively.

 - 18 years of age or older.

 


YOU HAVE

 - Previous retail or customer service experience preferred.

 - Strong communication skills and passion for building genuine customer connections.

 - Drive to achieve personal and team goals.

 - Willingness to support operational tasks as part of a team.

 - Availability to work a flexible, seasonal schedule including weekends and holidays.

 - Ability to stand/walk for entire shift and lift or move up to 25 lbs.

 - Willingness to follow our dress code, including wearing Rothy’s shoes during each shift.

 


OUR BENEFITS

 - Wellness and Commuter Programs

 - PTO and Wellbeing Time (where applicable)

 - Employee Discount Program

 

Pay Range: $17.00 – $18.00 per hour
Base pay is one part of our total rewards package. This role may be eligible for additional incentives. Pay is determined based on experience, skill, and location requirements.

source_jobs.title: Senior Handbag and Accessories Designer - Women’s
source_jobs.description:
Senior Handbag and Accessories Designer - Women’s

Location: San Francisco, CA (Hybrid)



At Rothy’s, we know there’s a better way to do business, and it starts by putting the planet and its people first. More than 200 million single-use plastic bottles and 715,000 pounds of ocean-bound marine plastic have been transformed into our signature thread, creating wardrobe staples that look just as good as they feel.



Striving for zero waste, we combine 3D knitting technology and handcrafted assembly to create machine washable styles that don’t compromise on comfort. From classic shoes to carry-everywhere bags, we create essentials for wherever you go.



At Rothy’s, building a sustainable future is at the heart of everything we do. We pride ourselves on fostering an inclusive environment at our HQ, retail stores and wholly-owned factory, and are growing our community every day.



About the Team:

The creative minds behind our beloved products. The Product Design team is a group of brilliant shoe and bag designers with unparalleled insight into past and future brand innovations. Whether designing all-new silhouettes or imagining fresh colors and patterns for existing styles, this powerhouse team dreams up the icons Rothy’s is known for—from yesterday, today and tomorrow.



About the role:

We’re looking to add a Senior Designer, specializing in women’s handbags and accessories, to our small but mighty Product Design Team. In this role, you’ll help develop our next Rothy’s product by collaborating on what pattern or color we should offer next! You’ll work closely with our creative Product Development Teams.



What you’ll do:

 - Design and assort handbag and accessories in both knit and non-knit materials from concept through production

 - Create, upload, and manage technical packages (tech packs) within the Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) system (Centric)

 - Research industry trends to inform accessory color, texture, and detail directions

 - Collaborate regularly with cross-functional teams—including Leadership, Merchants, and Design—throughout the development process

 - Track and maintain communication with factories for color, texture, and prototype development

 - Work closely with factories to resolve technical challenges related to knitting materials

 - Review prototypes and pre-production samples to ensure alignment with technical specifications

 - Support in the selection of colors in new materials

 - Create color and technical specifications for production

 - Contribute to the development of trims and novelty accessory components

 - Maintain accurate and up-to-date design files and databases

 - Manage color dip approvals

 - Create hardware tech packs

 - Maintain material, hardware and trim libraries

 - Organize and update the sample closet; recycle outdated samples.

 - Complete assigned tasks within established timeframes and adhere to the brand calendar

 

You are:

 - Collaborative with an enthusiastic mindset for learning

 - An excellent communicator with a proven ability to meet deadlines

 - Highly adaptable to a fast-paced, constantly evolving environment

 - Extremely organized with a meticulous attention to detail



You have:

 - 8 years of experience in knit color experience ideally in accessories

 - Expert proficiency in coloring and developing textures for knit materials

 - Demonstrated passion for accessories, color, pattern, knitwear, innovation and sustainability

 - Advanced skills in Photoshop and Adobe Illustrator

 - A deep understanding of textile color and material development

 - A strong grasp of product development practices

 - Experience creating industry-standard technical specifications

 - Familiarity with PLM systems, such as Centric



Our benefits:

 - Generous paid time off

 - 401k matching

 - Comprehensive health plans for you and your family

 - Supplemental mental health benefits

 - Monthly wellness reimbursement

 - Employee Discount Program!

At Rothy’s, building a sustainable future is at the heart of everything we do. We pride ourselves on fostering an inclusive environment at our HQ, retail stores and wholly-owned factory, and are growing our community every day.




ABOUT THE TEAM:

Our Real Estate team plays an essential part in bringing Rothy’s to life. From development to construction, these real estate gurus are responsible for expanding our physical footprint across the country—bringing our products to audiences in new and existing markets. We can’t wait to continue expanding our retail footprint, and the members of this team are leading the charge.




ABOUT THE ROLE:

As the Retail Construction Project Manager at Rothy’s you will be joining growing team that is leading our retail expansion efforts across North America. You will be responsible for managing successful new store build outs from design development to bidding, permitting to construction and finally hand-off to the store team. You will be integral to developing efficient processes and project schedules, managing budgets and cultivating a reliable vendor network in a high growth environment.

We believe in making women’s busy lives more seamless—and doing better for the planet while we’re at it. Be a part of the team that knows what’s coming next for Rothy’s!

If you’re eager to take on an array of responsibilities at a dynamic, fast-growing company, you just might be the perfect fit.






WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Drive all project schedules and manage contractors, vendors and consultants to meet all critical milestones in order to open all stores in accordance with the master schedule; regularly report out on progress and escalate issues as needed

 - Assist with due diligence on real estate sourcing to ensure building condition, dimensions, engineering, layouts and utilities are suitable to get the retail location up and running

 - Own the construction and FF&E budgets including preliminary cost estimates, budget forecasting, and cost management ensuring that the store build out is executed within all budget targets

 - Partner with the external architects to review the design and documentation to ensure it is in line with budget estimates and advise on any constructability issues that may impact schedule

 - Manage the GC bidding and appointment process, including qualifying and leveling of bids, as well as the award and contract execution

 - Oversee the procurement process for all vendors and suppliers to ensure on time and on budget appointment of all required vendors and suppliers

 - Negotiate and track all change orders in order to minimize the impact they have on the project budget

 - Establish a network of capable and effective vendors and contractors

 - Lead all weekly construction progress and procurement meetings in order to deliver punch list free stores to the Retail teams on time and under budget

 - Partner closely with Operations, Visual Merchandising and Retail teams throughout the store turnover process in order to complete cohesive and efficient store openings

 - Execute all project closeouts with a sense of urgency including punch lists and project specific closeout documents as required by landlords

 - Assist with the collection of the required documentation in order to allow for speedy collection of Tenant Improvement Allowances

ABOUT THE TEAM:

ABOUT THE ROLE:

As Store Manager, you create an exceptional customer experience while keeping daily operations running smoothly. You lead a high-performing, engaged team and ensure the store operates efficiently. You collaborate with other stores and departments to solve problems, drive improvements, and set your team up for success. As a Rothy’s brand ambassador, you enthusiastically bring the mission and values to life for our customers, employees, and community.




WHAT YOU’LL DO:

Team Leadership (35%)

 - Proactively recruit, hire, onboard and develop a strong store team

 - Develop team members through in-the-moment feedback, regular floor observations, and consistent 1:1 check-ins

 - Maintain a high performance standard by training the team on all SOPs, service expectations, selling skills, and Rothy's product knowledge

 - Lead the Division of Responsibility (DOR) program with your leadership team

 - Ensure consistent execution of the Manager on Duty (MOD) program by setting clear expectations, reinforcing accountability, and regularly reviewing performance to deliver seamless customer and employee experience

 - Coach for high performance and manage employee relations concerns in partnership with your supervisor and the People Team

 - Create a positive and motivating team culture by actively identifying and acting on engagement opportunities

Customer Experience (50%)

 - Lead and model Rothy’s Experience through exceptional service and selling from the sales floor, ensuring the team is fully service-certified and actively coaching, training, and inspecting behaviors to uphold brand standards

 - Build customer loyalty through effective clienteling, working together with the team to strengthen long term relationships

 - Be the store's expert on brand and product knowledge, ensuring the team confidently shares our style, fit, quality, and sustainability story

 - Drive sales and key performance indicators by setting clear goals, celebrating wins, and holding the team accountable through daily coaching

 - Plan and execute in-store events and activations that grow brand awareness, connect with the local community, and drive sales

 - Ensure all presentation touchpoints including visual merchandising, cleanliness, and dress code consistently reflect brand standards and optimize the in-store experience

Operational Excellence (15%)

 - Plan and execute weekly, monthly, and annual business strategies to hit sales growth and profit goals
